title: quercus pubescens var. congesta
intro: Quercus pubescens var. congesta, also known as the downy white oak, is a variant of the downy oak tree that belongs to the family Fagaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The bark, leaves, and roots of Quercus pubescens var. congesta have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced overall well-being. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to promote wound healing and skin health.
